476.reason: [The pattern "&" may match before the lexer has a chance to see if it's part of "&&", but since AND is declared before ERRORAND, this should not happen. However, if the input has a single "&", it should be caught here. The real issue might be that this token type is never reached because of an earlier match, but analysis shows no earlier token matches "&". Despite this, the test case fails, suggesting a flaw in error detection for single '&' or '|'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The pattern "&" will match the first '&' in "&&", causing incorrect tokenization before AND can be matched]
